clue: The wettest inhabited place on Earth is ___, a town in India that averages about 467 inches of rain a year.
The tallest known living tree on Earth is a coast redwood named ___, standing over 380 feet tall.
The oldest known individual living tree on Earth is a nearly 4,850-year-old bristlecone pine nicknamed ___.
The largest single living organism on Earth by weight is a Utah aspen grove named ___, all sharing one root system.
Despite its tree-like trunk, the banana plant has no wood and is actually the world's largest ___.
The Titan arum is nicknamed the corpse flower because when it blooms it reeks of ___.
The loudest sound in recorded history was the 1883 eruption of the volcano ___, heard nearly 3,000 miles away.
Because Earth bulges at the equator, the point on the surface farthest from Earth's center is not Everest but Mount ___ in Ecuador.
The largest desert on Earth is not the Sahara but ___, which qualifies because it gets so little precipitation.
Falling raindrops are not teardrop-shaped at all; larger ones are flattened on the bottom like a ___.
The highest wind gust ever recorded on Earth, 253 mph, was measured during a 1996 cyclone on ___, Australia.
The pleasant earthy smell when rain hits dry ground has a scientific name coined in 1964: ___.
In Death Valley's Racetrack Playa, heavy rocks mysteriously slide across the flat ground, pushed by thin sheets of ___.
A gas crater in Turkmenistan nicknamed the Door to Hell has been continuously on fire since the year ___.
High-altitude snow sometimes turns pink or red, a phenomenon called watermelon snow that is caused by ___.
